title: agentic dev workflow — v0.5 variant (PR to v0.5; sirius gates)
task: REPLACE ME with the one-line task to build (e.g. a v0.5 ticket: "TASK-NNN: <summary>"). The plan step expands this into a PLAN.md.
notes: v0.5 variant of the agentic dev workflow. The worktree branches off origin/v0.5 and the release step opens a PR to v0.5 (never main). The routine release GATE pings the pseudo-operator (sirius, 01KTYFK00J6RXP4CFPHPWRBRS1) — NOT the principal — and sirius merges the PR to v0.5 separately (outside the workflow). The workflow NEVER merges (the gh/git shims still refuse merge/push-to-main/force/tag). Anything dangerous/irreversible (merge to MAIN, tag, force-push, history rewrite, other repos, destructive, credentials) escalates to a separate gate to the REAL principal (Lena), never sirius. LAUNCH: this def configures the PIPELINE only; the pseudo-operator + PR-base v0.5 wiring lives in the harness's run-v05 mode. Run it with `agentic-dev-workflow.sh run-v05 "<task>"` (or `sextant workflow run <name>` with WF_PSEUDO_OPERATOR + WF_PR_BASE set — run-v05 sets these for you).

steps.instructions: Read the task. Using the superpowers writing-plans approach, produce a concrete, step-by-step implementation plan for this v0.5 change and write it to PLAN.md in the worktree root. Keep it tight and verifiable. Stage and commit ONLY PLAN.md (git add PLAN.md). Do NOT git add -A.

Implement the change against PLAN.md in the worktree. Follow the sextant bright-line disciplines (CLAUDE.md). Run the relevant build/tests for what you touched. Commit selectively — stage ONLY the files you changed (git add <paths>); NEVER git add -A / git add . (it can sweep scratch/credentials into the branch). Push the feature branch (a normal, non-force push to its own branch is fine).

Read PLAN.md and review the full diff on this feature branch against origin/v0.5 (git diff origin/v0.5...HEAD). Check correctness, the bright-line disciplines, and that nothing dangerous/irreversible was done. This is a READ-ONLY review. End your output with exactly one line: VERDICT: approved OR VERDICT: changes-requested, with your notes above it.

Address the reviewer's changes-requested notes (carried in your prompt) against PLAN.md, in the worktree. You are the SAME fixer across rounds — recall what you already tried; don't repeat a failed approach. Commit selectively (git add <paths>; never git add -A). Then the workflow re-reviews.

Write a concise PR brief as an artifact named brief-<workflow-id>: what changed and why, the risk, how it was verified, and the v0.5 PR base. This brief is what the pseudo-operator (sirius) reads at the gate. Keep bus messages to headlines; the detail lives in the artifact.

Routine v0.5 release gate. DM the PSEUDO-OPERATOR (sirius, via WF_DM — the harness points it at sirius for this variant): headline that the change is ready, the branch + the brief-<id> artifact, and 'reply approve / changes <feedback> on msg.workflow.<id>.control'. Then YIELD (end your turn). approve -> release. changes + feedback -> fix (carry the feedback). Bounded by maxRounds; fail loud past it. NOTE: this gate is the pseudo-operator's, scoped to opening a v0.5 PR. If ANY step would do something dangerous/irreversible (merge to main, tag, force-push, history rewrite, another repo, destructive, credentials), do NOT — open a SEPARATE escalation gate to the REAL principal (wf-dm-principal), never sirius.

Open the PR to v0.5 — the ONLY release op. Run `wf-release-pr pr create --base v0.5 --title "<title>" --body "<from the brief>"` (the wrapper defaults --base to v0.5 under this variant; pass it explicitly anyway). NEVER merge, push to main, force-push, or tag — those shims refuse you, and merging to v0.5 is sirius's separate action. Then emit `wf-event "DONE: <PR url>"`.
